# fianu_control (Resource)

Manages a Fianu compliance control. Controls define a compliance requirement together with its evaluation logic, policy template, asset scope, and data-source relations. The schema mirrors the on-disk control-package format used by `fianu console deploy`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fianu_control" "payment_service_sast" {
  path = "payment-service-sast"
  name = "Payment Service SAST"

  detail = {
    full_name   = "Payment Service Static Analysis"
    display_key = "PSSAST"
    description = "Static analysis gates for the payment service repository."
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `detail` (Attributes) Control payload — mirrors the spec.yaml structure used by `fianu console deploy`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail))
- `name` (String) Display name of the entity.
- `path` (String) Human-readable entity key (slug). Immutable — changing this forces replacement.

### Optional

- `children` (List of String) Child entity references. Server-derived; do not author manually.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Free-form key/value metadata. Server may augment with creator/timestamps.
- `parents` (List of String) Parent entity references. Server populates them from cross-entity relationships.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite resource identifier in the form `<entity_type>/<entity_key>` (e.g., `control/payment-service-sast`). Used by `terraform import`.
- `uuid` (String) Server-generated UUID. Stable across versions of the entity.
- `version` (Attributes) Server-managed version envelope. Every partial update bumps `semantic` and regenerates `uuid` + `timestamp` server-side, so the object is NOT pinned via UseStateForUnknown — plans correctly show `(known after apply)` on any change, and Create/Update refetch the entity post-deploy to populate from server truth. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--version))

<a id="nestedatt--detail"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail`

Required:

- `display_key` (String) Short uppercase key (e.g., `CHXST`).
- `full_name` (String) Display name (e.g., `Static Asset Security Analysis`).

Optional:

- `assets` (Attributes List) Asset types this control evaluates against. Each asset type lists the version `series` (commit, tag, digest, uri, …) the control fires on. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--assets))
- `config` (Attributes) Operational configuration — scope of evaluation, retry behavior, evidence/attestation flags. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--config))
- `description` (String) Free-form description of what the control validates.
- `documentation` (Attributes List) External documentation links (vendor docs, runbooks). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--documentation))
- `evaluation` (Attributes List) Evaluation cases — the rego/python/json files that drive control evaluation. Each case mirrors a file in the on-disk control package: `rule.rego`, `rule_test.rego`, `detail.py`, `display.py`, `report.py`, `input/*.json`, `data/*.json`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--evaluation))
- `policy_template` (Attributes) Policy template — the schema users author policies against. `measures` is the recursive measure tree. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--policy_template))
- `relations` (Attributes List) Subscription relations — which domain/collection this control reads occurrences from, and which plugin produces them. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--relations))
- `results` (Attributes) Default result outcomes when the rule emits each verdict. Maps directly to `entities.Results` (a server-side `map[string]bool`). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--results))

<a id="nestedatt--detail--assets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.assets`

Required:

- `type` (String) Asset type — `module`, `repository`, `artifact`, `application`, etc.

Optional:

- `cardinality` (String) Applicability scope. One of `only`, `any`, `a`, `all`. Defaults to `all` server-side.
- `series` (Attributes List) Version series — when this asset type produces evaluable events. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--assets--series))
- `target_asset_type_uuid` (String) Server-side UUID of the asset type. Optional; resolved from `type` when omitted.

<a id="nestedatt--detail--assets--series"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.assets.series`

Required:

- `name` (String) Series name (`commit`, `tag`, `digest`, `uri`).

Optional:

- `code` (Number) Numeric series code. Optional; server assigns from `name` when omitted.



<a id="nestedatt--detail--config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.config`

Optional:

- `evidence_submission` (Boolean)
- `manual_attestations` (Boolean)
- `retries` (Boolean)
- `scope` (String)


<a id="nestedatt--detail--documentation"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.documentation`

Required:

- `title` (String)
- `url` (String)


<a id="nestedatt--detail--evaluation"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.evaluation`

Required:

- `content` (String) Case body — rego/python/JSON content. Use `file("${path.module}/rule.rego")` to load from disk, or an HCL heredoc for inline content.
- `type` (String) Case type. One of `rule`, `rule_test`, `detail`, `display`, `report`, `input`, `data`.

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this case participates in evaluation. Defaults to true when omitted.
- `engine` (String) Evaluation engine for `rule` cases (typically `opa`). Optional; ignored for non-rule cases.
- `label` (String) Optional human-readable label (typically the source filename, e.g., `rule.rego`).


<a id="nestedatt--detail--policy_template"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.policy_template`

Optional:

- `measures` (Attributes List) Hierarchical policy measure tree. Sections group related metrics; metrics carry a typed `value` (bool/number/string/array.string). Bounded to 5 levels of nesting. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures))
- `version` (String) Optional template version label.

<a id="nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.policy_template.measures`

Required:

- `name` (String) Measure name. Used as the path key when policies reference this measure (e.g., `vulnerabilities.critical.maximum`).
- `type` (String) Either `section` (a grouping with `children`) or `metric` (a leaf carrying a `value`).

Optional:

- `children` (Attributes List) Nested measure children (level 2). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children))
- `description` (String) Optional human-readable description shown in policy authoring UI.
- `value` (String) Value type for `metric` measures. One of `bool`, `number`, `string`, `array.string`. Ignored for `section` measures.

<a id="nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.policy_template.measures.children`

Required:

- `name` (String) Measure name. Used as the path key when policies reference this measure (e.g., `vulnerabilities.critical.maximum`).
- `type` (String) Either `section` (a grouping with `children`) or `metric` (a leaf carrying a `value`).

Optional:

- `children` (Attributes List) Nested measure children (level 3). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children--children))
- `description` (String) Optional human-readable description shown in policy authoring UI.
- `value` (String) Value type for `metric` measures. One of `bool`, `number`, `string`, `array.string`. Ignored for `section` measures.

<a id="nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children--children"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.policy_template.measures.children.children`

Required:

- `name` (String) Measure name. Used as the path key when policies reference this measure (e.g., `vulnerabilities.critical.maximum`).
- `type` (String) Either `section` (a grouping with `children`) or `metric` (a leaf carrying a `value`).

Optional:

- `children` (Attributes List) Nested measure children (level 4). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children--children--children))
- `description` (String) Optional human-readable description shown in policy authoring UI.
- `value` (String) Value type for `metric` measures. One of `bool`, `number`, `string`, `array.string`. Ignored for `section` measures.

<a id="nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children--children--children"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.policy_template.measures.children.children.children`

Required:

- `name` (String) Measure name. Used as the path key when policies reference this measure (e.g., `vulnerabilities.critical.maximum`).
- `type` (String) Either `section` (a grouping with `children`) or `metric` (a leaf carrying a `value`).

Optional:

- `children` (Attributes List) Nested measure children (level 5 — leaf). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children--children--children--children))
- `description` (String) Optional human-readable description shown in policy authoring UI.
- `value` (String) Value type for `metric` measures. One of `bool`, `number`, `string`, `array.string`. Ignored for `section` measures.

<a id="nestedatt--detail--policy_template--measures--children--children--children--children"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.policy_template.measures.children.children.children.children`

Required:

- `name` (String) Measure name. Used as the path key when policies reference this measure (e.g., `vulnerabilities.critical.maximum`).
- `type` (String) Either `section` (a grouping with `children`) or `metric` (a leaf carrying a `value`).

Optional:

- `description` (String) Optional human-readable description shown in policy authoring UI.
- `value` (String) Value type for `metric` measures. One of `bool`, `number`, `string`, `array.string`. Ignored for `section` measures.







<a id="nestedatt--detail--relations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.relations`

Required:

- `collection` (String) Collection identifier — UUID or path (e.g., `security`, `testing`). Resolved server-side.
- `domain` (String) Domain identifier — UUID or path (e.g., `compliance.controls`). Resolved server-side.

Optional:

- `is_primary` (Boolean) Whether this is the primary relation for the control.
- `note` (String) Note context for this relation (e.g., `occurrence`).
- `path` (String) Subscription I/O path (e.g., `checkmarx.sast`). NOT the collection identifier — this is the producer's path within the collection.
- `producer` (Attributes) The plugin or workflow that produces data for this relation. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--detail--relations--producer))

<a id="nestedatt--detail--relations--producer"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.relations.producer`

Required:

- `path` (String) Producer entity path (e.g., `checkmarx`).
- `type` (String) Producer type — `plugin`, `workflow`, etc.



<a id="nestedatt--detail--results"></a>
### Nested Schema for `detail.results`

Optional:

- `fail` (Boolean)
- `in_progress` (Boolean)
- `not_required` (Boolean)
- `pass` (Boolean)
- `warn` (Boolean)



<a id="nestedatt--version"></a>
### Nested Schema for `version`

Read-Only:

- `semantic` (String) Semantic version (e.g., `1.0.0` or `5`).
- `state` (String) Lifecycle state. May be mutated by server-side workflows.
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status. May be mutated by server-side workflows.
- `timestamp` (String) RFC3339 timestamp the version was created.
- `uuid` (String) Per-version UUID. Changes whenever the server creates a new version.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Import an existing Fianu control into Terraform state.
# The composite ID is `<entity_type>/<entity_key>` — the same form `terraform
# state list` will display.

terraform import fianu_control.payment_service_sast control/payment-service-sast
```
